initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of lateral ischemia?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose lateral ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment elevation in lateral leads
Presence of J-point elevation in lateral leads
Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in lateral leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST-segment depression in at least two of the lateral leads, including leads I, aVL, V5, and V6? Note that ST depression is defined as a depression of the J-point greater than 0.07mV (0.7mm) in these leads.

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST-segment depression? Select all possible leads from the options below.

Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with ST-segment depression? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of lateral ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
